data.title: Pakistan Official: Houthi Attacks on Saudi Arabia Must Stop Before U.S.-Iran Truce
data.description: According to Jin10, a Pakistani official said the Houthis stopping attacks on Saudi Arabia and other Gulf states would be a prerequisite for resuming U.S.-Iran talks, and Pakistan has conveyed that message to Iran. Yemen's Houthi movement said on Thursday that it hit two Saudi oil tankers.

data.title: World Network Secures $52.5 Million In Fresh Funding To Fight Deepfakes
data.description: Sam Altman-backed World Network has secured $52.5 million in fresh funding as it shifts from basic network expansion to integrating its biometric screening devices across enterprise software suites. The project is now focusing entirely on that enterprise rollout, according to CoinDesk, as it aims to help combat online AI deepfakes.

data.title: Galaxy Research Cuts Clarity Act Passage Odds in 2026 to 30%
data.description: Galaxy Research has lowered its estimate that the Clarity Act becomes law in 2026 to 30%, down from 50% less than a month ago. According to NS3.AI, Alex Thorn said the bill needs a last-ditch effort as the Senate faces its August recess. Seven Democratic senators said the latest version still falls short.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: Vladimir Putin Says He Will Not Sell Weapons to Iran
data.description: According to Jin10, U.S. President Donald Trump said Russian President Vladimir Putin stated that he would not sell weapons to Iran.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump’s Saudi Nuclear Deal Draws Watchdog Interest
data.description:
Donald Trump’s plans for a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia are putting tensions between U.S. and South Korean reactor developers in the spotlight, according to Bloomberg.

The dispute has surfaced details of a clash over market access and drawn interest from regulators.

data.title: Pakistan Explores Ways to Restart U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description: According to three Pakistani sources, Pakistan is exploring ways to restart U.S.-Iran talks in an effort to end nearly five months of war. According to Jin10, exploratory talks were held during Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni's visit to Islamabad this week, his second trip in the past 10 days, and he met with Pakistani government leaders and army chief Asim Munir. According to the Pakistani government statement, Momeni is seen as having close ties to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps. The three sources also warned that obstacles to talks with the United States remain high.

data.title: Brazil's Central Bank Governor Says Many Studies Will Explore El Nino's Impact on CPI
data.description: Brazil's Central Bank Governor said many studies will explore El Nino's impact on CPI. According to Jin10, the governor made the remark without providing further details.

data.title: US Measles Cases Hit 35-Year High
data.description: US measles cases in 2026 have already topped last year’s total and reached a level not seen in more than three decades, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Brazil's Central Bank Governor: We Monitor Brent Crude Performance Daily
data.description: According to Jin10, Brazil's central bank governor said they monitor Brent crude oil performance every day.

data.title: Trump To Meet Netanyahu At White House On Tuesday
data.description: According to Axios, President Donald Trump will meet Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House on Tuesday, according to a statement from Netanyahu's office. The statement said Netanyahu will depart for Washington on Monday for an official visit, meet Trump at the White House on Tuesday and attend the funeral service for Senator Lindsey Graham. Axios reported that Netanyahu has met with Trump six times in the Oval Office since Trump returned to office about a year and a half ago, and that Trump is considering resuming major combat operations in Iran that could include a joint military campaign with Israel. Axios also reported that some White House officials said last week Netanyahu tried to "will the meeting into existence," and Trump told Axios on Thursday that he would meet Netanyahu if he was in town for Graham's funeral ceremony.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Cyclospora Outbreak Linked to Mexican Lettuce Spreads to Four More US States
data.description:
Federal health officials linked four additional states to a cyclosporiasis outbreak tied to tainted iceberg lettuce imported from Mexico, as the illness continues to spread across the US, according to Bloomberg.

The outbreak has sickened thousands of people and now includes Illinois, Kansas, Oklahoma and Pennsylvania.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Colombia Rations LNG as SPEC Port Shutdown Triggers Remote Work Urge
data.description:
Colombia’s government is urging schools and businesses to operate remotely for several days starting next week as the country prepares for natural gas rationing, according to Bloomberg.

The move comes during planned maintenance at the nation’s sole LNG import terminal, SPEC port.

data.title: U.N. Chief Deplores Houthi Attacks on Commercial Ships in the Red Sea
data.description:
U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres said he was deeply shocked by the Houthis’ renewed attacks on commercial ships transiting the Red Sea and their threats to maritime navigation. According to Odaily, the statement said the actions could further heighten regional tensions and draw Yemen deeper into the regional conflict.

The statement warned that the continued deterioration of the situation in Yemen would undermine prospects for peace and have serious economic, humanitarian, and environmental impacts across the region and beyond. Guterres called on all parties to de-escalate immediately and urged the Houthis to stop any further attacks or other escalation measures.

He also called on the Houthis to strictly comply with U.N. Security Council Resolution 2722 and subsequent resolutions on protecting commercial vessels and maintaining freedom of navigation in the Red Sea. Guterres said international navigation rights and freedom of navigation in and around the Bab el-Mandeb Strait must be fully restored.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Says U.S.-Iran Issue Is Not a Lack of Mediators
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said there is no problem of a lack of mediators between Iran and the United States, and that the issue lies in U.S. policy and practices. According to Jin10, Araghchi said Iran will not yield to U.S. pressure, will not accept threats, pressure, or coercive measures, and will continue to safeguard the interests of the Iranian people and the interests related to the Strait of Hormuz. He added that Iran will continue taking action until the legitimate goals and demands of the Iranian people are achieved.

data.title: UK PM Office: Burnham Says Britain Will Act More Actively If Markets and Regulation Fall Short
data.description: According to Jin10, the office of the UK prime minister said Prime Minister Burnham held talks with business groups and said Britain would act "more actively" if markets and regulation fail to meet expectations.

data.title: Japan's Gifu Hits 40 C As Heat Wave Reaches Fourth Straight Day
data.description: According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Xinhua, Japanese weather officials said temperatures reached 40 degrees Celsius in Mino, Gifu Prefecture, on Friday afternoon, marking the fourth consecutive day that Japan has recorded so-called "extreme heat days" and tying a historical record. Japan's Meteorological Agency this year defined temperatures of 40 degrees Celsius and above as "extreme heat days." The first such day this year was recorded on July 21 in Gujo, Gifu Prefecture, while temperatures of 40 degrees Celsius or higher were also observed in multiple locations on July 22 and 23. Japan has seen daytime highs above 35 degrees Celsius across the country in recent days, and the Fire and Disaster Management Agency said 10,857 people were taken to hospital for heatstroke emergencies in the week of July 13-19. Japanese media cited experts as saying a high-pressure system has created a "heat dome" over the country, trapping heat over the archipelago.

data.title: UN Urges Safe Evacuation of About 6,000 Seafarers Stuck In Strait Of Hormuz
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing AFP, United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk urged all sides in the Middle East conflict to help evacuate about 6,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. Türk told reporters in Geneva on Friday, July 24, that the parties to the conflict should urgently act to facilitate the safe departure and disembarkation of the seafarers and ensure the delivery of critical supplies.

The United Nations News, citing the latest update from the International Maritime Organization, said attacks on vessels resumed after hostilities restarted on July 12-13, and that there were now “virtually no ships” passing through the narrow waterway. IMO Secretary-General Arsenio Dominguez said he was deeply concerned about the 6,000 seafarers aboard about 500 anchored ships in the waters behind the Strait of Hormuz in the Persian Gulf. He said the workers were professionally trained but unarmed, and the ships were not equipped to withstand missile and drone attacks. Dominguez also said seafarers play a crucial role in global trade, noting that without them, 90% of shipped goods would not reach their final destinations.

As tensions between Iran and the United States continue, the question of how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed remains unresolved. United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Thursday that he regretted the escalating strikes and counterstrikes between the United States and Iran this month and warned that the situation was “spiraling out of control.”

data.title: Aspen Secures Rights to Develop Monthly Oral HIV Prevention Pill
data.description:
Aspen Pharmacare Holdings Ltd. secured the rights to develop, manufacture and sell what could become the world’s first monthly oral pill to prevent HIV, according to Bloomberg.

The deal positions South Africa at the forefront of efforts to combat the virus.

data.title: Kuwait Defense Ministry: Air Defense Systems Intercept Hostile Targets Launched From Iran
data.description: According to Jin10, Kuwait's air defense systems are currently intercepting hostile targets launched from Iran.

data.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, Meta and Palantir Urge U.S. Policymakers to Avoid Open-Weight AI Restrictions
data.description: According to CNBC, Nvidia, Microsoft, Meta, Palantir and more than 20 other companies on Friday urged policymakers to avoid premature restrictions on open-weight artificial intelligence models, saying such limits could stifle competition or push innovation overseas. The companies said open-weight models help competition and spread the benefits of AI more broadly, while warning that relying only on closed models is not inherently safe because they can be breached, misused or fail in ways outsiders cannot detect. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella shared the letter on their personal social media accounts. OpenAI and Anthropic did not sign the letter, even as both companies prepare for potentially large IPOs and have confidentially filed prospectuses with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
